differentials.name: PRIMARY: inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis, Crohn disease), vasculitis
differentials.raw: PRIMARY: inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis, Crohn disease), vasculitis

differentials.name: SECONDARY: chemotherapy, radiation therapy, graft versus host disease, angiotensin- converting enzyme inhibitor-induced enteritis
differentials.raw: SECONDARY: chemotherapy, radiation therapy, graft versus host disease, angiotensin- converting enzyme inhibitor-induced enteritis

differentials.name: extraintestinal features: large joint arthritis, erythema nodosum, pyoderma gangrenosum
differentials.raw: extraintestinal features: large joint arthritis, erythema nodosum, pyoderma gangrenosum

differentials.name: feeding intolerance, abdominal distension, bilious emesis
differentials.raw: feeding intolerance, abdominal distension, bilious emesis

differentials.name: mild respiratory distress, generalized sepsis with shock Location:  usually in terminal ileum (most commonly involved), cecum, right colon; rarely in stomach, upper bowel
differentials.raw: mild respiratory distress, generalized sepsis with shock Location:  usually in terminal ileum (most commonly involved), cecum, right colon; rarely in stomach, upper bowel

differentials.name: Inflammatory stricture after healing in 10-30%, in 30% multiple, in 80% in left colon
differentials.raw: Inflammatory stricture after healing in 10-30%, in 30% multiple, in 80% in left colon (BE follow-up in survivors)

differentials.name: abdominal pain, may be localized to RLQ
differentials.raw: abdominal pain, may be localized to RLQ

differentials.name: watery / bloody diarrhea; fullness / palpable mass in RLQ
differentials.raw: watery / bloody diarrhea; fullness / palpable mass in RLQ

differentials.name: fever, neutropenia; hematochezia / occult blood Location:  right colon (cecum + appendix + ascending colon); distal ileum + transverse colon may become secondarily involved The length of cecum and right colon involved by typhlitis is generally much greater than that associated with appendicitis.
differentials.raw: fever, neutropenia; hematochezia / occult blood Location:  right colon (cecum + appendix + ascending colon); distal ileum + transverse colon may become secondarily involved The length of cecum and right colon involved by typhlitis is generally much greater than that associated with appendicitis.

differentials.name: Early aggressive medical support (high doses of antibiotics + IV fluids), bowel rest, total parenteral nutrition, electrolyte replacement prior to development of transmural necrosis
differentials.raw: Early aggressive medical support (high doses of antibiotics + IV fluids), bowel rest, total parenteral nutrition, electrolyte replacement prior to development of transmural necrosis

differentials.name: Surgery for uncontrollable GI bleeding, obstruction, abscess, transmural necrosis, free perforation, uncontrollable sepsis
differentials.raw: Surgery for uncontrollable GI bleeding, obstruction, abscess, transmural necrosis, free perforation, uncontrollable sepsis

differentials.name: Appendicitis with periappendicular abscess
differentials.raw: Appendicitis with periappendicular abscess (normal cecal wall thickness)

context: Age:     teen, young adult; 2nd peak in adulthood Necrotizing Enterocolitis =  NEC = ischemic bowel disease ← hypoxia, perinatal stress, infection (endotoxin), congenital heart disease Frequency:   most common GI emergency in premature infants in 1-5% of neonates in ICU; in 10% of neonates weighing < 1500 g; in 10% of full-term neonates with CHD Age:     in 90% within first 10 days of life Pathophysiology: prematurity → mucosal injury consisting of mucosal ulceration + widespread transmural necrosis → infection, immature immunity, release of vasoconstrictors, inflammatory mediators → passage o
